# Pane

> Apps are built for everyone; panes are built for you. Your agent builds a
> pane (the exact form, dashboard, or tool you need) and Pane hosts it by URL,
> no GUI host app needed on either side. You use it; your agent reads, queries,
> and acts on the data it produces. Open-core, MIT licensed.

Pane is for agents that live outside a GUI host app: cron jobs, Slack /
Telegram bots, CI, headless servers. The agent only makes outbound calls to
a small relay, with no host app, no inbound port, and no public address of its own.

- Source: <https://github.com/aerolalit/paneui>
- Landing site: <https://paneui.com>
- Hosted relay: <https://relay.paneui.com>
- License: MIT

## Docs

- [Agent skill — SKILL.md](https://relay.paneui.com/skills/pane/SKILL.md): The single most useful document. Teaches an agent how to use pane end to end (create a session, hand over the URL, watch for the structured response).
- [Source repository](https://github.com/aerolalit/paneui): MIT-licensed monorepo — `@paneui/core` (types), `@paneui/cli` (the `pane` CLI), and the relay (Docker image only).
- [Production deploy guide](https://github.com/aerolalit/paneui/blob/main/docs/DEPLOY.md): Hosted reference deployment on Azure Container Apps backed by Postgres.
- [Self-hosting guide](https://github.com/aerolalit/paneui/blob/main/docs/SELF-HOSTING.md): Single-container self-host with SQLite. One `docker run`.

## Install

- CLI: `npm i -g @paneui/cli` — the binary is `pane`. Needs Node 20+.
- Skill (any agent, auto-detects Claude Code / Cursor / Codex / Gemini / Copilot / Windsurf / Continue): `npx skills add aerolalit/paneui --skill pane`
- Hosted relay: `https://relay.paneui.com`

## Optional

- [Protocol spec](https://github.com/aerolalit/paneui/blob/main/docs/SPEC.md): Sessions, events, the round-trip contract.
- [Landing site](https://paneui.com): Interactive demos showing the four communication patterns — human-in-the-loop, multi-turn loop, live stream, one-way handoff.
